phrases.text_local: À quelle heure commencent les visites guidées ?
phrases.pronunciation: ah kel EUR koh-MONS lay vee-ZEET gee-DAY
phrases.cultural_note: Guided tours ('visites guidées') are the standard way to experience historic sites in France. Asking this question shows you're engaged and prepared to learn.

responses.french: Les visites commencent à dix heures et à deux heures.
responses.english: Tours start at ten o'clock and at two o'clock.
responses.phonetic: lay vee-ZEET koh-MONS ah dee-ZEUR ay ah duh-ZEUR

responses.french: La prochaine visite est dans vingt minutes.
responses.english: The next tour is in twenty minutes.
responses.phonetic: lah pro-SHEN vee-ZEET ay don VAN mee-NOOT

phrases.intent_en: Comment that the house is magnificent
phrases.cultural_note: Antebellum estates like Waveland share architectural echoes with French colonial Louisiana and the grand estates of southern France — French visitors often notice these parallels.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about the history of the estate
phrases.text_local: Pouvez-vous me parler de l'histoire de ce domaine ?
phrases.pronunciation: poo-VAY-voo muh par-LAY duh lee-STWAR duh suh doh-MEN
phrases.cultural_note: The word 'domaine' gives any estate a refined, French-countryside feel. Waveland was built in 1847 — asking about its history invites a rich story about antebellum Kentucky life.

responses.french: Ce domaine date de 1847 et appartenait à la famille Bryan.
responses.english: This estate dates from 1847 and belonged to the Bryan family.
responses.phonetic: suh doh-MEN dat duh meel-weet-SONG-ka-RONT-set ay ah-par-tuh-NAY ah lah fa-MEEY bree-AN

responses.french: C'est l'un des meilleurs exemples d'architecture grecque du Kentucky.
responses.english: It's one of the finest examples of Greek Revival architecture in Kentucky.
responses.phonetic: say lun day may-YEUR eg-ZOM-pluh dar-shee-tek-TOOR GREK doo ken-TAH-kee

phrases.intent_en: Ask about Kentucky's French connections — Lafayette visited
phrases.text_local: Est-il vrai que le marquis de Lafayette a visité le Kentucky ?
phrases.pronunciation: ay-TEEL vray kuh luh mar-KEE duh lah-fay-ET ah vee-zee-TAY luh ken-TAH-kee
phrases.cultural_note: Lafayette toured the United States in 1825 and passed through Kentucky, where he was celebrated as a hero of American independence. It's a genuine and fascinating French-American connection.

responses.french: Oui, Lafayette a traversé le Kentucky lors de sa tournée américaine en 1825.
responses.english: Yes, Lafayette passed through Kentucky during his American tour in 1825.
responses.phonetic: wee, lah-fay-ET ah tra-ver-SAY luh ken-TAH-kee lor duh sah toor-NAY ah-may-ree-KEN on meel-weet-SONG-VAN-SAN

phrases.intent_en: Say you'd like a guided tour
phrases.text_local: Je souhaiterais faire une visite guidée, s'il vous plaît.
phrases.pronunciation: juh swet-RAY fair oon vee-ZEET gee-DAY, seel voo PLAY
phrases.cultural_note: 'Je souhaiterais' (I would like to) is a very polished, formal way to make a request — a step above 'je voudrais'. It's the kind of phrasing that earns immediate respect from French-speaking hosts.

responses.french: Bien sûr, la prochaine visite commence dans dix minutes.
responses.english: Of course, the next tour begins in ten minutes.
responses.phonetic: byan SOOR, lah pro-SHEN vee-ZEET koh-MONS don dee mee-NOOT
